Military Mar 31, 2026

More A-10s Are Headed to the Middle East

The deployment of additional A-10Cs to the Middle East, the first of which arrived at RAF Lakenheath, demonstrates the continued reliance on the close air support aircraft for operations in Iran. The U.S. Air Force A-10C Thunderbolt II jets have been actively supporting Operation Epic Fury in Iran...

The A-10's persistent deployment to the Middle East underscores the Air Force's continued dependence on legacy platforms for sustained close air support despite decades-old aircraft design.

See The Aviationist →

Regulatory Mar 31, 2026

Trump International? One Final Hurdle Left For Palm Beach Airport's Renaming

The name change hasn't been finalized yet, as it still requires FAA approval.

Airport rebranding requires FAA coordination beyond local government approval, creating extended timelines for stakeholders planning signage and operations updates.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

IndiGo names ex-British Airways boss and current IATA head Willie Walsh as CEO

Low-cost-carrier IndiGo has named the former British Airways boss and current director general of International Air Transport Association…...

Walsh's appointment signals IndiGo's transition from rapid growth to operational maturity and international expansion under proven legacy airline leadership.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 12 Airlines That Still Operate 4-Engine Passenger Jets

Only three families of passenger quadjets remain in service around the world with a contracting number of commercial airlines.

As quadjet operators dwindle, remaining airlines must justify high fuel costs and maintenance complexity against modern twin-engine efficiency, limiting this fleet to ultra-long-range and high-capacity niche routes.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Safety Mar 31, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from investigating accidents post-facto to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before incidents occur, fundamentally transforming safety culture.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Poland rules out Patriot redeployment after US request reported

The United States has informally asked Poland to consider redeploying one of its two Patriot air defense batteries…...

NATO ally resistance to US air defense requests may signal shifting burden-sharing dynamics affecting allied force posture and procurement decisions.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

China targets 140 launches in 2026 amid commercial space surge

China plans to conduct around 140 orbital launches this year, according to a commercial launch executive, marking a sharp acceleration in the country’s launch cadence.

China's 140-launch target in 2026 signals aggressive commercialization of space infrastructure, potentially reshaping global satellite launch pricing and supply chain competition.

See SpaceNews →

International Mar 31, 2026

Iran war forces IATA to cut global seat capacity growth projections

The International Air Transport Association (IATA) has said that global capacity growth eased to 3.3% year on year…...

Geopolitical instability is forcing the aviation industry to moderate near-term expansion expectations, signaling broader economic uncertainties affecting fleet deployment strategies.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Massive 25-Hour Flights: Qantas' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Huge changes are coming to Qantas' longest services. See them all here!

Qantas' ultra-long routes will require operational innovations in crew scheduling, aircraft maintenance windows, and passenger comfort standards that may set new industry benchmarks.

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Effective Safety Management Systems depend on psychological safety and blame-free reporting cultures, which require sustained leadership commitment beyond procedural compliance.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

AI drones are now catching aerial threats like flying cops

XTEND is doubling down on one of the biggest challenges in modern warfare: how to stop hostile drones, without making things worse in the process. The AI-driven robotics company, backed by Eric Trump, has announced a new partnership with ParaZero Technologies as part of its broader $1.5 billion...

AI-enabled drone interception systems are moving beyond theoretical development into commercial partnerships, creating a nascent market segment for counter-UAS technology amid escalating airspace security concerns.

See DroneDJ →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Two EA-37Bs Arrive at RAF Mildenhall on Possible First Operational Deployment

Two EA-37B Compass Call aircraft stopped at RAF Mildenhall for fuel during what may be the type’s first operational deployment abroad, reports suggesting Turkey as their destination. Two U.S. Air Force EA-37B Compass Call electronic warfare aircraft have arrived at RAF Mildenhall, UK, on Mar. 31,...

The EA-37B Compass Call's potential first operational deployment signals U.S. commitment to electronic warfare capabilities in contested regions, likely supporting NATO ally Turkey amid regional tensions.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Europe’s space sector faces power shift as funding grows

New report from Aerospace Corp. highlights rising EU funding and defense focus...

Increased EU space investment signals European aerospace suppliers should expect stronger government contracts and potential regulatory alignment shifts away from commercial priorities.

See SpaceNews →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

DroneShield opens Amsterdam HQ as counter-drone demand grows

Drone threats aren’t slowing down in Europe, and neither is DroneShield. The Australia-based counter-drone and electronic warfare specialist has officially opened its new European headquarters in Amsterdam, signaling a deeper, more permanent push into one of its fastest-growing markets. more…...

DroneShield's European expansion reflects how counter-drone technology is transitioning from niche military capability to critical civilian infrastructure protection across commercial airspace.

See DroneDJ →

▶
Safety Mar 31, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit confusion at LAX reveals a systemic design issue where pilot expectations misalign with ground markings, highlighting the need for standardized taxiway designation protocols at major hubs.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Embraer KC-390 Millennium showcases cold weather operations in Sweden

Embraer’s KC-390 Millennium demonstrator has completed short takeoff and landing operations at Sweden’s Vidsel Test Range as part of the…...

KC-390 Millennium's Arctic demonstration validates tactical airlift capability for NATO allies in high-latitude operations, expanding Embraer's addressable market among European defense customers.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Sphere ditches contractors, speeds up HubT drone deployments

Sphere is tightening its grip on autonomous drone deployments with a set of practical upgrades to its HubT platform, but the biggest shift is how the system is now built. more…...

Sphere's vertical integration of HubT drone operations removes intermediaries, potentially reducing deployment latency and improving operational margins for autonomous delivery services.

See DroneDJ →

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Researchers say SAF made from solar energy could be scaled to industrial levels

The University of Sheffield has developed a new way of making sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) using solar energy…...

Solar-derived SAF production achieving industrial scalability could reduce aviation's feedstock dependency on agricultural biomass and fossil fuels simultaneously.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

UK drone rules just got way easier to understand

Grey Arrows Drone Club just did something UK drone pilots have quietly needed for years. And if you’ve ever second-guessed where you’re allowed to take off, this could be a game-changer. more…...

Simplified UK drone regulations reduce compliance friction and may accelerate recreational and commercial drone adoption by eliminating interpretation ambiguity.

See DroneDJ →

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strikes remain a top-three fatal helicopter cause despite pilots' overconfidence in reaction time, indicating a critical gap between perceived and actual risk in low-altitude operations.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 5 Airlines With The Largest Boeing 737 Fleets Today

Boeing's single-aisle narrowbody family has been a popular choice for many years.

Southwest's outsized 737 dependency (750+ aircraft) creates concentrated operational and supply chain vulnerability compared to competitors with diversified fleets.

Military Mar 31, 2026

Rheinmetall and Boeing Australia formalize MQ-28 Ghost Bat bid

Germany’s interest in acquiring the MQ-28 Ghost Bat has moved from diplomatic signals to a formal industrial partnership in a…...

Germany's formal partnership with Boeing Australia on the MQ-28 Ghost Bat signals NATO's accelerating demand for autonomous combat systems, creating significant procurement opportunities for defense contractors.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

MiniV-Bat 234g, 74 minutes hover bicopter

Bicopters are theoretically the most efficient multi-rotor design for their size constraint, yet their real-world...

Bicopters achieve superior efficiency-to-weight ratios compared to traditional quadcopters, enabling extended endurance missions with minimal payload penalties.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

2026 might be the most affordable year to buy a DJI drone

DJI drones that once felt like serious investments are now showing up at prices that would’ve seemed unrealistic even a year ago. Thanks to the Amazon Big Spring Sale, models across the lineup are seeing steep discounts, opening the door at just $149 and making it very easy to imagine how different...

DJI's aggressive pricing strategy in 2026 signals intensifying competition in the consumer drone market, potentially reshaping entry barriers and market penetration rates.

See DroneDJ →

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation systems offers a near-term pathway to environmental sustainability while handling projected traffic growth without major infrastructure expansion.

See MDPI →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Drone survey without GCPs? Wingtra claims sub-3 cm accuracy

Wingtra is stepping into 2026 with a clear message for the surveying world: the future of mapping might not need ground control points at all. The Switzerland-based drone company recently launched the SURVEY61 payload, designed to work with the WingtraRAY drone. And the pitch is hard to ignore —...

Wingtra's elimination of ground control points through advanced payload technology could dramatically reduce surveying costs and timelines while maintaining professional-grade accuracy standards.

See DroneDJ →

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Sikorsky Collaborates with Robinson Helicopter Company to Integrate MATRIX™ Autonomy into Robinson R66 TURBINETRUCK

Sikorsky, a Lockheed Martin company (NYSE:LMT), and Robinson Helicopter Company (RHC) unveiled the R66 TURBINETRUCK, a game-changing...

Autonomous helicopter integration into established platforms like the R66 enables existing operators to upgrade capabilities without aircraft replacement, reducing operator transition costs.

See sUAS News →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Artemis 2 countdown underway

The two-day countdown for the Artemis 2 mission around the moon started March 30 with NASA officials reporting no major issues for the launch.

Artemis 2's smooth countdown progression signals NASA's readiness to resume crewed lunar missions after years of delays and technical challenges.

See SpaceNews →

Corporate Mar 31, 2026

ICE Eyes 10 New Boeing 737 And Gulfstream G650s For Deportation Fleet

The controversial agency is jet shopping again.

ICE's aircraft acquisition strategy signals sustained demand for business aviation in government operations despite political controversy surrounding the agency.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Inside The Boeing 747's Upper Deck Layout

From a secluded cocktail bar to a high-density seating zone, the evolution of the 747's upper deck reflects the changing priorities of aviation.

The 747's upper deck transformation from luxury lounge to revenue-generating seats reveals how airlines shifted from premium experience differentiation to maximizing seat-mile economics.

See Simple Flying →

General Aviation Mar 31, 2026

Fires Tear Through Nebraska Grasslands

Dry, warm, and windy conditions across the U.S. Great Plains led to extreme fire activity in March 2026.

Grassland fires across the Great Plains may impact low-level flight operations and visibility for general aviation aircraft in affected regions.

Military Mar 31, 2026

5 Fighter Jets With The Highest Top Speeds In Service Today

From the Mach 2.83 MiG-31 to the F-22, these are the 5 fastest fighter jets still in service—and why speed still matters in modern air combat.

Modern air superiority depends on speed for initial engagement and escape, explaining why nations maintain hypersonic interceptors despite advanced missile technology dominating combat.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

JetBlue Hikes Checked Bags Fees, Cites "Rising Operating Costs"

JetBlue announces a hike checked bag fees, balancing the need to keep fares competitive with the reality of escalating operations costs.

JetBlue's fee increase reflects a strategic shift toward ancillary revenue as fuel and labor costs compress margin recovery through base fares alone.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

5 Routes That Push Aircraft To Their Range Limits

These services offer extra-long-range connectivity.

Ultra-long-range routes demand specialized aircraft and fuel planning strategies that airlines use to justify premium positioning and network expansion into underserved markets.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Second Starlink satellite suffers anomaly, generating debris

For the second time in just over three months, a SpaceX Starlink satellite has generated debris from an apparent on-orbit malfunction.

Recurring Starlink anomalies underscore growing orbital debris risks from mega-constellations, forcing aviation and aerospace operators to adapt tracking and collision avoidance protocols.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Delta Slashes Domestic Boeing 767-400 Flights By 67% In Massive Schedule Shakeup

The airline will redeploy a significant portion of its 767-400ER fleet from domestic routes to services to and from Europe, affecting several routes.

Delta's redeployment of 767-400s to transatlantic routes signals airlines are optimizing wide-body capacity toward higher-margin international markets as domestic demand stabilizes.

See Simple Flying →
